answers.alpha: see solution_steps (function of geometry and loads)

key_concepts: general plane motion of pinned rod
kinematics of rigid body (a_G from α)
Newton's 2nd law for center of mass
solution_steps: Geometry: rod at 40° → G at (L/2·cos40°, L/2·sin40°) from A = (0.574, 0.482) m
B at (L·cos40°, L·sin40°) = (1.149, 0.964) m from A
ΣM_A = I_A·α (CCW+):
Torque from P (horizontal, at B): P·(y_B) = 30·0.964 = 28.92 N·m CCW
Torque from weight (downward at G): −m·g·(x_G) = −39.24·0.574 = −22.52 N·m
By acts vertically at B. But By is unknown — need another equation.
Actually with roller at B (vertical reaction only), By is unknown too.
Use ΣM_A=I_A·α, ΣFx=m·a_Gx, ΣFy=m·a_Gy and kinematics: a_G = α × r_AG (ω=0 initially).
At this instant ω=0: a_Gx = −α·(L/2)·sin40° = −α·0.482; a_Gy = α·(L/2)·cos40° = α·0.574
Wait — if α is CCW, a_G = α×r_AG: a_Gx = −α·(L/2·sin40°), a_Gy = α·(L/2·cos40°)
Three equations, three unknowns (α, By, Ax, Ay) — actually 4 unknowns. One more: constraint that B stays on surface → a_By = 0 (vertical acc of B).
a_By = a_Ay + α×r_AB (vertical component). Since A is fixed: a_B_vertical = α·(L·cos40°)·...
a_Bx = −α·L·sin40°; a_By = α·L·cos40° (with CCW positive)
Constraint: a_By = 0 (B on frictionless surface, roller — WRONG, roller only constrains vertical force not acceleration unless the surface is horizontal and B stays on it)
Actually if B rests on horizontal frictionless surface, a_By = 0 (B must stay on surface).
a_By = α·L·cos40° = 0 → α·1.5·0.766 = 0 → α = 0 only if B stays on surface.
This is a constraint that as long as contact is maintained. At this instant a_By = 0.
α·1.149 = 0 → this contradicts non-zero P. This means the rod lifts off B immediately.
NOTE: This problem is more complex than stated. The roller at B may lift off. Treat By ≥ 0 as a KKT condition.
Assuming B stays on surface: α = 0 and the system is in instantaneous statics — contradicts the dynamics problem intent.
REVISED INTERPRETATION: There is no roller at B. The rod is pinned only at A. P is applied at B.
Without roller: 3 unknowns (α, Ax, Ay), 3 equations.

answers_corrected.alpha: 2.13 rad/s² (CCW), assuming no roller constraint at B
